name: Beef Stifado
recipeInstructions:
SERVE WITH PILAFF, SEASAME COATED BREAD, SALAD W/FETA
1. Cut meat into 1-inch chunks.
2. Melt butter in a large frying pan. Brown the meat.
3. Transfer the meat to a crockery pot.
4. Stir drained onions and brown sugar into the frying pan.
5. Cook over medium-high heat, shaking the pan, until glazed. Transfer to the crockery pot.
6. Add wine, water, tomato paste, and vinegar to the frying pan. Stir to loosen drippings. Pour over meat and onions.
7. Place pickling spices and cloves in a tea ball or cheesecloth bag. Add to the pot.
8. Season with minced garlic, salt, and pepper.
9. Cover and cook on LOW for 8 hours.
10. Thaw peas. Add during the last half hour of cooking.
11. Cook down juices until slightly reduced.
Makes 8 servings. NOTE: Pickling spices and red wine vinegar enhance this Greek stew. Complement the Aegean munu with pilaff, sesame-coated bread, and salad with feta cheese.
